summary: Completed comprehensive testing of all four P1 features for PR Review Bot: (1) Rate limiting on webhook endpoint working correctly - triggers 429 after 31 requests, (2) Webhook retry/dedup handling working - same X-GitHub-Delivery header returns {duplicate: true}, (3) Custom analysis rules/configuration working - disabled_rules, enabled_rules, and severity_overrides all functional, (4) Multi-repo support with per-repo settings - full CRUD operations working including authentication requirements and edge cases. Fixed bug in /api/analyze-cpp endpoint to support config parameter.

fixed_bugs.issue: Missing config parameter support for custom rule configuration
fixed_bugs.fix_applied: Added config field to CppAnalyzeRequest model and passed to analyze_cpp function

passed_tests: POST /api/github-webhook with same X-GitHub-Delivery header twice returns {duplicate: true} and does NOT create duplicate webhook_log entry
POST /api/github-webhook rate limiting - sending 35 rapid requests from same repo returns 429 after 31 requests
POST /api/github-webhook for repo with enabled=false in repo_settings returns 'Analysis disabled' message
GET /api/available-rules returns list of all 14 C++ rule names
POST /api/repo-settings creates new repo config with all fields (enabled, auto_post_comments, rate_limit_rpm, severity_threshold, rule_config)
GET /api/repo-settings lists all configured repos
GET /api/repo-settings/{owner}/{name} for configured repo returns saved settings
GET /api/repo-settings/{owner}/{name} for unconfigured repo returns defaults with is_default=true
PUT /api/repo-settings/{owner}/{name} partial update works (only updates provided fields)
DELETE /api/repo-settings/{owner}/{name} removes settings and returns success
DELETE /api/repo-settings/{owner}/{name} for non-existent repo returns 404
POST /api/repo-settings for already-existing repo returns 409 conflict
All repo settings endpoints require authentication (return 401 without cookie)
C++ analyzer with rule_config {disabled_rules: ['endl_flush']} does NOT produce endl_flush findings
C++ analyzer with rule_config {enabled_rules: ['pass_by_value']} ONLY produces pass_by_value findings
C++ analyzer with severity_overrides {shared_ptr_overhead: 'high'} changes that finding's severity
All existing endpoints still work: auth login, webhook ping, stats, reviews, analyze-cpp, preview-comment
All 14 C++ performance analysis rules working correctly
Authentication system working: login, logout, session management, brute force protection
Diff parser handling multiple file types and formats correctly
Comment formatting and preview system working correctly
